was a football league established by the [Gibraltar Football Association](/wiki/Gibraltar_Football_Association "Gibraltar Football Association") (GFA) in 1905, and the top tier of [football in Gibraltar](/wiki/Football_in_Gibraltar "Football in Gibraltar"). It originally contained eight member clubs, but it has grown over the years. In 1909 the league was split into two divisions between which clubs can be [promoted and relegated](/wiki/Promotion_and_relegation "Promotion and relegation") at the end of each season. In the 2013–14 season, the Premier Division contained eight clubs with the [Second Division](/wiki/Gibraltar_Second_Division "Gibraltar Second Division") containing 12 clubs. For sponsorship reasons it is known as the **Argus Insurance Premier Division**.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Former association football league in Gibraltar

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This article **needs additional citations for [verification](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help [improve this article](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Gibraltar_Premier_Division "Special:EditPage/Gibraltar Premier Division") by [adding citations to reliable sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners"). Unsourced material may be challenged and removed. *Find sources:* ["Gibraltar Premier Division"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Gibraltar+Premier+Division%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Gibraltar+Premier+Division%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Gibraltar+Premier+Division%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Gibraltar+Premier+Division%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Gibraltar+Premier+Division%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Gibraltar+Premier+Division%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(June 2015)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

Football league

Gibraltar Premier Division

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Founded | 1895 |
| Folded | 2019 |
| Country | Gibraltar |
| [Confederation](/wiki/Geography_of_association_football "Geography of association football") | [UEFA](/wiki/UEFA "UEFA") |
| Number of clubs | [10](#Premier_Division_2018–19_Season) |
| Level on pyramid | 1 |
| [Relegation](/wiki/Promotion_and_relegation "Promotion and relegation") to | [Gibraltar Second Division](/wiki/Gibraltar_Second_Division "Gibraltar Second Division") |
| Domestic cup | [Rock Cup](/wiki/Rock_Cup "Rock Cup") |
| International cup(s) | [UEFA Champions League](/wiki/UEFA_Champions_League "UEFA Champions League") [UEFA Europa League](/wiki/UEFA_Europa_League "UEFA Europa League") |
| Most championships | [Lincoln Red Imps](/wiki/Lincoln_Red_Imps_F.C. "Lincoln Red Imps F.C.") (24 titles) |
| Website | [Gibraltar FA](http://www.gibraltarfa.com) |

The **Gibraltar Premier Division** was a football league established by the [Gibraltar Football Association](/wiki/Gibraltar_Football_Association "Gibraltar Football Association") (GFA) in 1905, and the top tier of [football in Gibraltar](/wiki/Football_in_Gibraltar "Football in Gibraltar"). It originally contained eight member clubs, but it has grown over the years. In 1909 the league was split into two divisions between which clubs can be [promoted and relegated](/wiki/Promotion_and_relegation "Promotion and relegation") at the end of each season. In the 2013–14 season, the Premier Division contained eight clubs with the [Second Division](/wiki/Gibraltar_Second_Division "Gibraltar Second Division") containing 12 clubs. For sponsorship reasons it is known as the **Argus Insurance Premier Division**.

From the 2014–15 season, as a result of the territory's membership of [UEFA](/wiki/UEFA "UEFA"), the champions entered the first [UEFA Champions League](/wiki/UEFA_Champions_League "UEFA Champions League") qualifying round. The winners of Gibraltar's cup competition, the [Rock Cup](/wiki/Rock_Cup "Rock Cup"), meanwhile, gained entry to the following season's [UEFA Europa League](/wiki/UEFA_Europa_League "UEFA Europa League") qualifying rounds. From the 2016–17 season, Gibraltar received an additional place in the Europa League qualifying round for the league runner-up. In 2019 the league merged with the [Gibraltar Second Division](/wiki/Gibraltar_Second_Division "Gibraltar Second Division") and became the [Gibraltar National League](/wiki/Gibraltar_National_League "Gibraltar National League").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Gibraltar_Premier_Division&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

[Football in Gibraltar](/wiki/Football_in_Gibraltar "Football in Gibraltar") dates from 1892, when military personnel in the territory started playing as [Prince of Wales](/wiki/Prince_of_Wales_F.C. "Prince of Wales F.C."). By 1895 the number of clubs had grown to the point that the [Gibraltar Football Association](/wiki/Gibraltar_Football_Association "Gibraltar Football Association") was formed and a tournament (the predecessor to the Premier Division), the Merchants Cup, began, with [Gibraltar](/wiki/Gibraltar_F.C. "Gibraltar F.C.") winning the inaugural competition. By 1907 the Premier Division had officially taken shape with Prince of Wales winning the first full season of league football in Gibraltar. The side went on to win 19 league titles before folding some time in the 1950s.

Since the league's formation, football has been played almost every season, along with the [Gibraltar Second Division](/wiki/Gibraltar_Second_Division "Gibraltar Second Division") (founded 1909) and a national cup competition, the [Rock Cup](/wiki/Rock_Cup "Rock Cup"). After the FA's admission to [UEFA](/wiki/UEFA "UEFA"), the [Gibraltar Premier Cup](/wiki/Gibraltar_Premier_Cup "Gibraltar Premier Cup") was established in 2013 to provide more competitive games for the sides in the top tier.

Due to the restricted and amateur nature of the league in the past, the competition has been prone to spells of dominance at certain times: for example, the early 20th century dominance by Prince of Wales and the 10 league titles in 11 seasons attained by [Glacis United](/wiki/Glacis_United_F.C. "Glacis United F.C.") in the 1960s and 1970s. At present [Lincoln Red Imps](/wiki/Lincoln_Red_Imps_F.C. "Lincoln Red Imps F.C.") are the dominant side in the division, having won every season since 2000–01 with the exception of the 2001–02 season, until 2017. However, the UEFA acceptance and potential for increased professionalism in the league has opened the possibility of other sides closing the gap, with sides such as [Manchester 62](/wiki/Manchester_62_F.C. "Manchester 62 F.C.") and [Lynx](/wiki/Lynx_F.C. "Lynx F.C.") making inroads in the 2013–14 season. This came to pass in the [2016–17](/wiki/2016%E2%80%9317_Gibraltar_Premier_Division "2016–17 Gibraltar Premier Division") season, when [Europa FC](/wiki/Europa_F.C. "Europa F.C.") won their first title in over 60 years.

In 2018, the Gibraltar FA announced changes to the league involving an increase in the "Home Grown Player" quota to 3 players on the pitch, per team, at any one time, as well as introducing salary caps (to be introduced in the senior leagues from 2019) and the introduction of the [Gibraltar Intermediate League](/wiki/Gibraltar_Intermediate_League "Gibraltar Intermediate League"), replacing the Reserves League and Under-18 League, in order to promote local player development.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

For the 2019–20 season, the Premier Division and the Second Division were merged to create a single division for Gibraltar, known as the [Gibraltar National League](/wiki/Gibraltar_National_League "Gibraltar National League").[[3]](#cite_note-3)
